reasoning: The Town Hall stands ALONE at the center with a proper civic plaza. No buildings crowd it — plots 1-4 are breathing room. Visitors arrive at the center and see the clock tower rising above an open plaza, with roads radiating outward. This is the heart from which all infrastructure grows.
vision: The Town Square (plots 1-4) should stay mostly open. As the town grows past 20+ buildings, a civic complement could go here — a library, post office, or fountain. But never crowd the Town Hall. The plaza IS the infrastructure.

reasoning: The IVProduced Tech Corner is a tech-focused developer hub with strong visual identity (dark slate, NVIDIA green, Copilot blue glow). Plot 17 on the North Road west side (x=21.5, z=8) places it mid-way along the residential north lane, adding commercial diversity. Its dark-mode facade and glowing monitors make it a striking landmark when approaching from Main Street.
vision: The North Road gains its first tech landmark. Devs wandering north from Main Street discover the Copilot-sparkle roofline glowing at the end of the lane — a beacon for builders.

reasoning: The Arasaka pop-up kiosk is placed on the North Road east side (plot 18, x=28.5, z=8) — tucked between Bobby's House and Hilbert's Hotel along the quiet residential lane. A glossy corporate kiosk that clearly wandered in from a megacity mall feels perfectly out-of-place nestled in the neighborhood, which is exactly the vibe the contributor requested.
vision: The North Road gains a delightfully incongruous corporate intruder. Residents heading to Hilbert's Hotel pass a suspiciously spotless kiosk offering 'Personal Security Solutions for Everyday Life', survey drone hovering hopefully outside.

reasoning: The B is a neon-drenched arcade bunker themed around Dragon's Lair. Main Street East entertainment plots (10-14) are fully occupied, so this unique dark-facade landmark gets plot 29 (x=4, z=15) at the far west outskirts near the junction of Main Street and North Road. Its dramatic midnight-blue exterior and glowing neon marquee will draw visitors from the road as a distinctive landmark.
vision: Plot 29 gives The B room to be a standalone spectacle — its neon glow visible from the Main Street West strip. The hot pink and teal neon tubes contrast dramatically with the surrounding village aesthetic, making it an unmissable destination for anyone exploring the western edge of town.
